title: feat: add cap on out-of-diff issue comments to prevent rate limiting
body:
## Background

The `postOutOfDiffFindings()` function posts each out-of-diff finding as a separate GitHub issue comment. Unlike review comments which are batched in a single API call, issue comments require individual HTTP requests.

## Problem

For PRs with many out-of-diff findings (e.g., adding a new linter rule to legacy code), this creates O(N) API calls which could trigger GitHub's secondary rate limits (403/429).

## Proposed Solution

Add a configurable cap (default: 10) on individual out-of-diff comments:
- First N findings get posted as individual issue comments (visible to MCP tools)
- Remaining findings get aggregated into the summary comment (existing behavior)

This maintains O(1) API complexity while preserving MCP visibility for the most important findings.

## Implementation Notes

Location: `internal/usecase/github/poster.go` in `postOutOfDiffFindings()`

```go
const maxOutOfDiffComments = 10

// Post individual comments up to cap
for i, pf := range outOfDiffFindings {
    if i >= maxOutOfDiffComments {
        // Remaining go to summary
        break
    }
    // ... existing posting logic
}
```

Consider making the cap configurable via `review.maxOutOfDiffComments` in bop.yaml.

## Related

- Identified during review of #261
- Original implementation in commit a1a7f56 (v0.8.1)
